title: centaurea amara subsp. gaudinii
intro: Centaurea amara subsp. gaudinii is a plant species within the aster family, scientifically known as Centaurea, and specifically referred to as the bitter knapweed or gaudin's knapweed. The plant's roots and flowers have been traditionally used for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of Centaurea amara subsp. gaudinii include treating digestive issues, reducing fever, and alleviating respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of the plant are often available in tinctures, infusions, and teas, which can be used to support overall health and well-being.
